g.text: COUNT-MIN SKETCH: 'how many times did item X appear in the stream?'

text.tspan: STRUCTURE: a 2D array of counters: d rows u00d7 w columns
d = number of hash functions (rows)
w = number of counters per row
Each row has its own independent hash function
UPDATE(x, count):
for each row i in 1..d:
j = hash_i(x) mod w
counters[i][j] += count
(item x lands at one cell per row, hashed independently)
QUERY(x): estimate the count of x
for each row i in 1..d:
j = hash_i(x) mod w
candidates.append(counters[i][j])
return MIN(candidates) # MIN reduces overestimate
The MIN works because collisions only ADD to counts;
the smallest of d candidate counters has the fewest collisions.
PROPERTIES:
u2022 NEVER UNDERESTIMATES (some collisions inflate counts)
u2022 Error bound: with w=O(1/u03b5) and d=O(log 1/u03b4),
overestimate u2264 u03b5 u00d7 total_stream_count with probability 1-u03b4
u2022 Heavy hitters: keep a top-K list updated alongside sketch
u2022 Mergeable: add cell-wise across sketches u2192 distributed counting
REAL-WORLD USES:
u2022 streaming TOP-K (most-frequent IPs, URLs, words)
u2022 DDoS detection: 'which IPs have made > 10k requests this minute?'
u2022 network monitoring: heavy-flow detection in routers
u2022 NLP: frequency tables in fixed memory (word2vec preprocessing)
u2022 Twitter: trending-topic detection alongside HLL for unique users
u2022 Cassandra: internal sketch for compaction decisions
